index.title: PENGER v1.0 — Final Backup for Digital Wealth
index.metaDescription: PENGER v1.0 — Aerospace-grade titanium seed phrase storage. A premium physical backup for digital capital owners who think in decades.
index.ogDescription: Indestructible titanium vault for your seed phrase. Secure your crypto capital for generations with an error-proof binary grid.
index.keywords: titanium seed phrase storage, crypto backup, BIP39 metal plate, cold storage, seed phrase vault, self-custody, bitcoin backup, hardware wallet backup, metal seed phrase plate, fireproof crypto backup, seed phrase metal plate, titanium crypto storage, indestructible seed backup, cryptocurrency seed storage, seed phrase engraving plate
index.heroTagline: Paper burns. Hard drives fail. Memory fades.
index.heroSubheadline: A titanium backup plate for your seed phrase that you encode yourself
index.vulnerabilityH2:
Digital assets are only forever
if your access is forever.
index.vulnerabilityText: A piece of paper in a drawer or a text file on a hard drive is a single point of failure waiting to happen.
index.antiFeat3: Titanium safe. Financial will. Absolute cold storage.
index.privacyH2: Trust no one. Secure it yourself.
index.privacyDesc1: Your seed phrase never leaves your hands. Encode and stamp it fully offline — on your terms.
index.privacyDesc2: Zero digital components. No batteries, no Bluetooth, no WiFi. Nothing to hack, nothing to update, nothing to fail.
index.privacyDesc3: Every word maps to a public BIP39 index. Verify your backup with any compatible wallet — no PENGER software needed.
index.howH2: From seed phrase to permanent record.
index.howStep1Desc: Find the BIP39 index for every word in your seed phrase using our Dictionary or any BIP39 wordlist.
index.howStep2Desc: Convert each index to a 12-bit binary sequence using the Simulator, a reference chart, or manual calculation.
index.howStep3Desc: Use the center punch tool to mark each 1-bit on the titanium grid. One column per word, top-to-bottom. Done.
index.academyDesc: Practical, jargon-light guides on seed phrases, wallets, key management, and operational security. Each article covers both beginner fundamentals and advanced technical depth.
index.guideSeedDesc: What is a mnemonic seed phrase, where do the words come from, and why does their order matter? From CSPRNG entropy to BIP39 checksum and master key derivation.
index.guideWalletDesc: Understand the difference between a wallet and an address, how public and private keys work, and how HD wallets derive millions of addresses from a single seed.
index.guideSovereigntyDesc: The philosophy and practice of being your own bank. Why self-custody matters, the risks of custodial services, and a practical roadmap to true financial autonomy.
index.guideComparisonDesc: Hot vs cold, hardware vs software, custodial vs self-custodial. When to use each type, and how multi-signature and air-gapped signing raise the security floor.
index.guideOpsecDesc: Threat modeling, physical backup hygiene, digital footprint minimization, and key ceremony protocols. Practical steps to protect what you hold.
index.guidePassphraseDesc: How an optional BIP39 passphrase adds an extra security layer on top of your seed phrase, enables hidden wallets, and provides plausible deniability under duress.
index.guideMultisigDesc: Multi-key setups that eliminate single points of failure. Quorum policies, vendor diversity, collaborative custody, and Taproot-based privacy improvements.
index.orderDesc: One titanium plate. One center punch. One complete encoding guide. Everything you need to permanently back up your seed phrase — no electronics, no third parties, no compromises.
index.kitDesc: The complete kit includes everything you need to permanently secure your seed: Titanium Plate · Puncher · Practice Template · Pencil · Security Sleeve.
index.featTitaniumDesc: Heat-resistant, water-resistant, and corrosion-proof. Built to survive extreme conditions and outlast you.
index.featBinaryDesc: Standard letter-punching leads to human error. We use an error-resistant binary grid for maximum precision.
index.featDegradationDesc: No electronic components to fail. Metal doesn’t forget.
index.featPrivacyDesc: If someone accidentally sees the plate, your seed phrase is not immediately readable. The binary layout acts as a physical layer of privacy.
index.protocolDesc: A BIP39 seed phrase is fundamentally a number system. Penger v1.0 doesn’t just record words; it archives them as raw, mathematical data.
index.stepLocateDesc: Find each word of your 12-word or 24-word recovery phrase in the provided printed dictionary.
index.stepPunchDesc: Use the included puncher to mark the plate corresponding to your words. Every row is one word. You do this entirely offline.
index.stepRecoverDesc: To read the backup in the future, simply calculate the total for each row. The sum gives you the exact index number in the BIP39 word list.
index.eduAcademyDesc: In-depth guides on self-custody and security
index.encodingH2:
From Words to Titanium.
A Complete Guide.
index.enc01: 2,048 words — every BIP39 seed phrase draws from a standardized word list.
index.enc02: Index (1–2048) — each word maps to a unique number, encoded as 12-bit binary.
index.enc03: Punch the plate — that 12-bit code is what gets permanently stamped into titanium.
index.encStep1Text: Your wallet generates a seed phrase from a standardized dictionary of 2,048 English words. Each word has a fixed position (index) in the list.
index.encStep2Text: Look up each word in the printed BIP39 dictionary included in the kit, which shows the index number next to each word.
index.encStep3Text: Convert the index to an 11-bit binary number: filled cell = 1, empty cell = 0.
index.encStep4Text: Each row on the titanium plate represents a word; use the puncher to mark positions where the bit is 1, leaving the others blank.
index.simCalloutText: Use our free online simulators to practice encoding and decoding before using the plate — generate a test seed phrase, see how binary encoding works, and verify your understanding
index.manifestoH2: Built by engineers who understand self-custody.
index.manifestoText: We believe the future of finance is personal ownership. But personal ownership is impossible without reliable, autonomous, and physically independent seed storage.
index.ctaH2:
You have done everything to secure your capital.
Except the final step.
index.ctaSub: Stop risking your digital wealth on paper. Control your seed. Control your future.

simulators.title: Simulators — PENGER
simulators.metaDescription: Interactive BIP39 seed phrase tools. Generate random mnemonics with SHA-256 checksum and decode binary dot patterns back into words. Runs 100% offline in your browser.
simulators.keywords: BIP39 generator, seed phrase simulator, binary encoder, mnemonic decoder, crypto tools, PENGER simulators, seed phrase generator online, BIP39 word list tool, mnemonic phrase generator, binary seed encoder, offline crypto tool, seed phrase practice tool
simulators.genDesc: Generate a random BIP39 mnemonic seed phrase with SHA-256 checksum. Each word is mapped to a binary index (1–2048) and converted to a dot pattern for metal plate backup. Supports 12, 15, 18, 21 and 24-word phrases.
simulators.dotMatrixTitle: Seed Phrase Dot Matrix for Metal Plate
simulators.dotMatrixDesc: Each column represents one word from your seed phrase. A filled dot means punch (binary 1), empty means skip (binary 0). Rows are bit weights from 2048 (top) to 1 (bottom).
simulators.wordTableDesc: Click any row to see how the BIP39 word index is converted to 12-bit binary and how bit weights sum to the index number.
simulators.decDesc: Decode binary dot patterns back into BIP39 seed phrase words. Click dots on the grid to input a pattern — each column decodes into a mnemonic word in real time. Valid indices: 1–2048.
simulators.inputGridDesc: Click a dot to toggle between punched (1) and empty (0). Each column encodes one seed phrase word. Rows represent bit weights from 2048 (top) to 1 (bottom).
simulators.warningText: Never enter real seed phrases into this or any online simulator, and do not use phrases generated here for actual crypto wallets. Online tools cannot guarantee security against malware or compromised devices. Always use an air-gapped hardware wallet for real seed generation and backups.
simulators.faq1q: What is a BIP39 seed phrase and why does it matter?
simulators.faq1a: A BIP39 seed phrase is a set of 12 to 24 words from a standardized 2048-word list. It is the master backup of your cryptocurrency wallet — from these words, all your private keys can be derived. Losing your seed phrase means losing access to your funds permanently, which is why a durable, offline backup on a Penger titanium plate is essential.
simulators.faq2q: How does seed phrase to binary conversion work?
simulators.faq2a: Each BIP39 word maps to an index number between 1 and 2048. This index is encoded as a 12-bit binary value. Each bit (1 or 0) becomes a dot on the metal plate — filled means punch, empty means skip. This is exactly the format used on the Penger plate, making it easy to encode your full seed phrase in a compact, tamper-evident pattern.
simulators.faq3q: Why store a seed phrase on a metal plate instead of paper?
simulators.faq3a: Paper backups degrade over time and are vulnerable to fire, water, and physical damage. A titanium plate can survive temperatures above 1,600°C, is waterproof, and resists corrosion. The Penger plate uses a binary dot pattern format that is compact, tamper-evident, and can encode a full 24-word seed phrase on a single plate.
simulators.faq4q: Is this seed phrase generator secure?
simulators.faq4a: This tool runs 100% in your browser — no data is ever sent to any server. Random generation uses the Web Crypto API for cryptographically secure randomness, and the page works fully offline. However, never use any generated seed phrase for a real wallet. For actual wallet backups, always use your hardware wallet’s built-in seed generator and store the result on a Penger titanium plate.
simulators.faq5q: What do 12, 15, 18, 21 and 24-word seed phrases mean?
simulators.faq5a: The word count determines the entropy (randomness) of your seed. 12 words = 128 bits, 15 = 160 bits, 18 = 192 bits, 21 = 224 bits, 24 words = 256 bits. More words mean stronger security. Most hardware wallets use 12 or 24 words. The last word includes a SHA-256 checksum to catch transcription errors. The Penger plate supports all word counts up to 24.
simulators.faq6q: How do I read the dot pattern on a metal plate?
simulators.faq6a: Each column on the plate represents one seed word. The 12 rows correspond to bit weights: 2048, 1024, 512, … 4, 2, 1 (top to bottom). A punched dot = 1, empty = 0. Add up the bit weights for all punched positions to get the word index, then look it up in the BIP39 dictionary. Use the Decoder tab above to practice reading patterns before working with your Penger plate.
simulators.faq7q: What is the difference between the Generator and the Decoder?
simulators.faq7a: The Generator creates a random BIP39 seed phrase with a valid SHA-256 checksum and converts each word to its binary dot pattern. The Decoder works in reverse — you input a binary dot pattern by clicking dots on the grid, and it converts each column back into the corresponding BIP39 word. Together they let you practice encoding and decoding seed phrases before committing to a permanent Penger titanium backup.
simulators.faq8q: Can I use this tool offline?
simulators.faq8a: Yes. All computation happens entirely in your browser using JavaScript. No network requests are made — the page even blocks fetch, WebSocket, and XMLHttpRequest APIs as a privacy hardening measure. You can disconnect from the internet before using the tool for an extra layer of security.
simulators.faq9q: What is a SHA-256 checksum in a seed phrase?
simulators.faq9a: The last word in a BIP39 seed phrase is not fully random — part of it is a checksum derived from the SHA-256 hash of the entropy bits. This checksum allows wallets to detect transcription errors. If you change even one word, the checksum will not match and the phrase will be flagged as invalid — another reason to store your phrase on a durable Penger plate rather than relying on handwritten copies.
simulators.faq10q: Why should I never enter my real seed phrase into any website?
simulators.faq10a: Even if a website claims to be offline and secure, your device may be compromised by malware, keyloggers, or malicious browser extensions that can capture everything you type. Your seed phrase is the master key to all your funds — once exposed, your cryptocurrency can be stolen instantly and irreversibly. Always generate and handle real seed phrases only on trusted hardware wallets, and back them up physically on a Penger titanium plate.
simulators.faq11q: What is the BIP39 word list and where does it come from?
simulators.faq11a: BIP39 (Bitcoin Improvement Proposal 39) defines a standard list of 2048 English words used to encode wallet entropy as a mnemonic phrase. The words were carefully chosen to be unambiguous — no two words share the same first four letters, making them easy to identify even if partially damaged. You can browse the full list on our Dictionary page.
simulators.faq12q: Can I use any words as a seed phrase?
simulators.faq12a: No. Only the 2048 words from the BIP39 standard list are valid. Each word maps to a specific index (1–2048), and the last word contains a SHA-256 checksum that depends on all the others. A random set of English words would almost certainly fail checksum validation. Browse the full list on our Dictionary page.
simulators.faq13q: What is the difference between a seed phrase and a private key?
simulators.faq13a: A seed phrase is a human-readable master backup from which all your private keys are mathematically derived. One seed phrase can generate billions of private keys through hierarchical derivation paths (BIP32/BIP44). Losing a seed phrase means losing access to every address derived from it — making a permanent backup on a Penger titanium plate critical.
simulators.faq14q: How many possible seed phrases exist?
simulators.faq14a: A 12-word seed phrase has 128 bits of entropy — approximately 3.4 × 1038 possible combinations. A 24-word phrase has 256 bits — about 1.16 × 1077 possibilities. For comparison, the estimated number of atoms in the observable universe is around 1080. Brute-forcing a valid seed phrase is computationally infeasible with any current or foreseeable technology.
simulators.faq15a: A passphrase (sometimes called the “25th word”) is an optional extra string you can add on top of your seed phrase for additional security. Unlike seed words, it can be any text. Adding a passphrase creates a completely different set of derived keys and wallet addresses. If you forget the passphrase, you will not be able to access the funds — even with the correct seed phrase. Store both your seed phrase and passphrase hint securely, for example on separate Penger plates.
simulators.faq16q: What happens if I enter an invalid pattern in the Decoder?
simulators.faq16a: If a column results in an index of 0 (all dots empty) or greater than 2048, it will be flagged as an invalid word. The status badge shows VALID when all word indices fall within the 1–2048 range, or INVALID otherwise. The Decoder maps individual columns to words — it does not verify the SHA-256 checksum of the full phrase.
simulators.footerDisclaimer: This BIP39 seed phrase tool runs fully offline in your browser — no data is transmitted or stored. For real cryptocurrency wallet backups, always use your hardware wallet's built-in seed generator and verify independently.

aboutUs.title: About Us — PENGER
aboutUs.metaDescription: PENGER builds aerospace-grade titanium seed phrase storage for cryptocurrency self-custody. Learn about our mission, team, and product.
aboutUs.keywords: PENGER about, titanium seed phrase, crypto backup company, self-custody team, PENGER team, seed phrase backup manufacturer, titanium plate company, crypto security company
aboutUs.heroH1: Physical Sovereignty for the Digital Age
aboutUs.heroDesc: We build tools that bridge the gap between digital wealth and physical permanence. No clouds. No servers. No third parties. Just titanium and mathematics.
aboutUs.originP1: PENGER was born from a simple realization: the crypto industry had solved private key generation, wallet encryption, and transaction signing — but left the physical backup as an afterthought. We set out to engineer a backup solution with the same rigor applied to the cryptography itself.
aboutUs.originP2: The result is an aerospace-grade titanium plate with a binary encoding system that eliminates human error, resists extreme environments, and requires zero digital tools to create or restore.
aboutUs.missionText: We believe that true ownership means holding your own keys — and that holding your own keys means having a backup you can trust for generations. Our mission is to make seed phrase security accessible, permanent, and error-proof for every digital asset holder.
aboutUs.principle1Desc: No apps, no Bluetooth, no WiFi, no firmware. The encoding process is entirely offline and manual. Your seed phrase never exists in digital form during backup.
aboutUs.principle2Desc: Grade 2 titanium withstands 1,668°C, resists corrosion, and survives conditions that destroy every other backup medium. Built to outlast the owner.
aboutUs.principle3Desc: The BIP39 binary grid with built-in checksum verification eliminates transcription errors. Every word maps to an 12-bit sequence — unambiguous, verifiable, permanent.
aboutUs.principle4Desc: Built on the BIP39 standard used by every major hardware and software wallet. No proprietary formats, no vendor lock-in. Your backup works with the entire ecosystem.
aboutUs.productP1: Each PENGER kit includes a precision-machined titanium plate, a center punch tool, and a complete encoding guide. The plate features a 12-word binary grid aligned with the BIP39 wordlist — 2,048 words mapped to 11-bit binary sequences.
aboutUs.productP2: The encoding process is simple: look up each word's index, convert to binary, and punch the corresponding dots. No electronics, no software, no third party ever sees your phrase. Restoration follows the same process in reverse.
aboutUs.educationP1: Security without understanding is fragile. That's why we built a comprehensive educational platform alongside our product. The PENGER Academy covers everything from seed phrase anatomy and wallet architecture to operational security and multisig setups.
aboutUs.educationP2: Our interactive simulators let you practice binary encoding without risk. The BIP39 dictionary provides instant word lookups. And our AI Tutor answers crypto security questions in real time.
aboutUs.educationAcademy: In-depth guides on self-custody and security
aboutUs.leaderBio: Building physical sovereignty tools for the digital age. The vision behind PENGER is simple: your keys, your crypto, your responsibility — and the backup should be as resilient as the conviction.
aboutUs.ctaDesc: Explore our product, learn through the Academy, or get in touch with any questions.

cookie.bannerText: We use strictly necessary cookies to run the site, and — with your consent — analytics and marketing cookies to measure performance, analyze behavior, and serve ads.
cookie.learnMore: Learn more in our Cookie Policy
cookie.necessaryDesc: Required for the site to function correctly. Cannot be disabled.
cookie.analyticsDesc: Helps measure visits, clicks, behavior, and traffic quality.
cookie.marketingDesc: Used for ad pixels, remarketing, and measuring ad campaigns.

cookiePolicy.title: Cookie Policy | PENGER
cookiePolicy.metaDescription: PENGER cookie policy — learn how we use cookies and similar technologies, what data we collect, which services are involved, and how to manage your consent.
cookiePolicy.keywords: cookie policy, PENGER cookies, website cookies, privacy, tracking, analytics cookies, marketing cookies, consent management
cookiePolicy.heroDesc: This policy explains in full detail how PENGER (mypenger.com) uses cookies and similar technologies when you visit our website. We believe in radical transparency: you deserve to know exactly what data is collected, how it is processed, where it goes, and who has access. We use strictly necessary, analytics, and marketing technologies — each described below with full technical detail. Analytics and marketing technologies are only activated after you give explicit consent.
cookiePolicy.whatH2: What Are Cookies and Similar Technologies?
cookiePolicy.whatCookies: Cookies are small text files placed on your device by websites you visit. They are widely used to make websites work efficiently, remember your preferences, and provide information to site owners.
cookiePolicy.whatLocalStorage: Local storage and similar technologies (such as localStorage and sessionStorage) serve a similar purpose — they allow the website to store data locally on your device. Unlike cookies, they are not sent with every HTTP request and can hold more data.
cookiePolicy.whatPurpose: On this website, we use these technologies to ensure the site functions correctly, to remember your consent choices, and — with your permission — to measure site usage and deliver relevant advertising.
cookiePolicy.cat1Desc: Required for the website to function and to save basic user settings (language preference, consent choice). These technologies are always active and cannot be disabled through the consent toggle. They do not track you across websites and do not send any data to third parties. Specifically, we store: your language preference (lang in localStorage), your consent choice (penger_consent in localStorage), and your order configuration (penger_order in sessionStorage — temporary data used to carry your selected product options between checkout pages, automatically cleared when you close the browser tab or after a successful purchase).
cookiePolicy.cat2Desc: Used to measure visits, events, user behavior, and traffic quality. These technologies are only activated after you give explicit consent. Analytics data helps us understand which pages are visited, how users navigate the site, and where they experience friction. We use this data solely to improve the website experience — we do not build personal profiles or sell this data. All analytics data is aggregated and we do not attempt to identify individual users.
cookiePolicy.cat3Desc: Used for advertising measurement, remarketing, and matching ad campaigns. These technologies are only activated after you give explicit consent. The "strictly necessary" exception does not apply to advertising purposes. Marketing technologies allow ad platforms to measure whether an ad click led to a website visit or purchase, and to show you relevant ads on other platforms. When enabled, these technologies may set cookies that can track your activity across different websites.
cookiePolicy.svc1Purpose: Tag management container. Controls loading of analytics and marketing tags based on user consent via Google Consent Mode v2. GTM itself does not track users — it acts as a dispatcher that loads or blocks other scripts depending on your consent state. The consent defaults are set to 'denied' for all categories before GTM initializes.
cookiePolicy.svc1Data: Consent state signals (analytics_storage, ad_storage, ad_user_data, ad_personalization), tag configuration events. GTM does not collect personal data on its own — it only reads the consent state and decides which tags to fire.
cookiePolicy.svc1Storage: No cookies set by GTM itself (container script only). The GTM JavaScript file is loaded from googletagmanager.com.
cookiePolicy.svc1Loaded: Always (manages consent signals for other tags)
cookiePolicy.svc2Purpose: Web analytics — measures site traffic, page views, events, user engagement, and traffic sources. GA4 uses an event-based data model: every interaction (page view, click, scroll, etc.) is recorded as an event with parameters. Data is sent to Google's servers where it is aggregated into reports.
cookiePolicy.svc2Data: Page URL and title, referrer URL, campaign parameters (utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, utm_term, utm_content), session ID, client ID (random identifier), screen resolution, viewport size, browser name and version, operating system, device category (desktop/mobile/tablet), language setting, approximate geolocation (country/city level, derived from IP), page load timing, scroll depth percentage, engagement time (how long the page was in focus), outbound link clicks, file downloads, site search terms, custom events we configure (e.g. button clicks, product configuration changes), and ecommerce events: view_item (when you view the product page), begin_checkout (when you start checkout), purchase (on successful payment — includes order ID, order total, currency, and product details such as finish, quantity, and selected options), and generate_lead (a conversion event sent alongside purchase for ad campaign optimization — includes order value and currency). Your IP address is used by Google to derive approximate geolocation but is not stored in GA4 reports (IP anonymization is enabled by default in GA4).
cookiePolicy.svc2Storage: _ga — client identifier cookie, randomly generated, used to distinguish users. Expires after 2 years of inactivity. Format: GA1.1.XXXXXXXXXX.TIMESTAMP. _ga_* — session state cookie tied to the specific GA4 property. Expires after 2 years. Stores session count and timestamp of first/last visit. _gid — daily user identifier. Expires after 24 hours. Used to group events within a single day.
cookiePolicy.svc3Purpose: Session behavior analysis — session recordings, heatmaps, and behavioral analytics. Clarity records how users interact with the page (where they click, how they scroll, what they hover over) and plays it back as an anonymized session recording. This helps us identify UX issues such as dead clicks, rage clicks, and confusing navigation. Requires valid consent signal for EEA/UK/CH traffic per Microsoft's Consent Mode integration.
cookiePolicy.svc3Data: Clicks (coordinates, target element), scroll depth and direction, mouse/touch movement coordinates, page views and navigation paths, session recordings (DOM snapshots with all text input fields automatically masked — no passwords, emails, or personal text is captured), rage clicks (repeated fast clicks indicating frustration), dead clicks (clicks on non-interactive elements), JavaScript errors, page load performance, viewport size, device type. Clarity does not record keystrokes in input fields.
cookiePolicy.svc3Storage: _clck — Clarity user identifier. Expires after 1 year. _clsk — Clarity session grouping cookie. Expires after 1 day. CLID — long-term identifier for linking sessions. ANONCHK — checks whether session data was properly transferred. MR — Microsoft cookie for referral tracking. MUID — Microsoft user identifier (set by bing.com domain). SM — used in synchronizing MUID across Microsoft domains. Some of these cookies are set on the microsoft.com/bing.com domains (third-party context).
cookiePolicy.svc4Purpose: Tracking visitor activity and ad measurement — advertising conversions, remarketing audiences, and campaign effectiveness across Meta platforms (Facebook, Instagram). When loaded, the Meta Pixel sends a PageView event to Meta's servers. This allows Meta to: (1) measure whether ad clicks led to visits on our site, (2) build remarketing audiences (e.g., show ads to people who visited our site), and (3) optimize ad delivery. The Pixel can track across websites if the user is logged into Facebook/Instagram.
cookiePolicy.svc4Data: Page URL, referrer, PageView events, custom conversion events (e.g. Purchase, Lead, InitiateCheckout — sent when you complete a purchase or start checkout, including order value and currency for ad measurement), HTTP headers (user agent, Accept-Language), Meta cookie identifiers (_fbp, _fbc), Facebook click identifier (fbclid from URL if present), browser and device information, IP address (used by Meta for geo-targeting and then hashed), screen resolution, and Facebook Login status if applicable. Important: Meta may combine this data with your Facebook/Instagram profile to show targeted ads.
cookiePolicy.svc4Storage: _fbp — browser identifier set by Meta Pixel on our domain (first-party). Format: fb.1.TIMESTAMP.RANDOM. Expires after 90 days. Used to identify the browser for ad attribution. _fbc — click identifier, set when a user arrives from a Facebook ad (contains the fbclid parameter). Expires after 90 days. fr — third-party cookie set by facebook.com domain for ad delivery and measurement. Expires after 90 days. Additionally, Meta may set cookies on the facebook.com domain that are outside our control.
cookiePolicy.dataH2: What Data and Signals Are Collected
cookiePolicy.dataText: Below is a comprehensive and transparent breakdown of every type of data that may be collected when you visit our website. We want you to know exactly what is captured, by whom, and for what purpose.
cookiePolicy.dataSubhead1: Always collected (strictly necessary, no consent required):
cookiePolicy.data1: Language preference — your selected language (en/uk), stored in localStorage on your device. Never sent to any server.
cookiePolicy.data2: Consent state — your cookie consent choices (which categories you accepted/rejected), stored in localStorage under the key penger_consent. Includes timestamp, policy version, and per-category status. Never sent to any external server.
cookiePolicy.data3: Google Consent Mode signals — when GTM loads, it reads your consent state and sets Google Consent Mode parameters (analytics_storage, ad_storage, ad_user_data, ad_personalization) to 'granted' or 'denied'. These signals are sent to Google to control tag behavior.
cookiePolicy.data4: Page views — the URL and title of every page you visit on our site, sent to Google Analytics and Microsoft Clarity.
cookiePolicy.data5: Referrer — the URL of the page that linked you to our site (e.g., a Google search results page or a social media post).
cookiePolicy.data6: Campaign parameters (UTM) — if you arrived via an ad or campaign link, the UTM tags in the URL (utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, utm_term, utm_content) are captured by Google Analytics.
cookiePolicy.data7: Session information — a randomly generated session ID, session start time, number of sessions, and whether this is your first visit. Used to group your page views into a single browsing session.
cookiePolicy.data8: Client identifiers — randomly generated IDs stored in cookies (_ga, _ga_*, _gid, _clck). These are not your real identity — they are random strings used to distinguish one browser from another. Example: GA1.1.1234567890.1710000000.
cookiePolicy.data9: Device and browser information — device category (desktop, mobile, tablet), operating system name and version, browser name and version, screen resolution, viewport size, device language setting.
cookiePolicy.data10: Approximate geolocation — your country and city, derived from your IP address by Google and Microsoft. Your IP address is not stored in Google Analytics 4 reports. Microsoft Clarity uses IP for session-level geo but does not expose it in dashboards.
cookiePolicy.data11: User interactions — clicks (which elements you click on), scroll depth (how far down you scroll expressed as a percentage), engagement time (how long the page was actively in the browser foreground), outbound link clicks (links that take you away from our site), file downloads.
cookiePolicy.data12: Mouse and touch movement — Microsoft Clarity records cursor movement coordinates and touch gestures to generate heatmaps and session recordings. This shows where users tend to look and interact on the page.
cookiePolicy.data13: Session recordings — Microsoft Clarity captures a replay of your browsing session as a series of DOM changes. All text typed into input fields is automatically masked and not recorded. The recording shows page layout, scrolling, clicks, and navigation — not personal input.
cookiePolicy.data14: UX quality signals — Clarity detects rage clicks (repeated fast clicks on the same area), dead clicks (clicks on non-interactive elements), excessive scrolling, and JavaScript errors. These help us identify broken or confusing UI elements.
cookiePolicy.data15: Page performance — page load timing, time to first byte, DOM content loaded time. Helps us optimize site speed.
cookiePolicy.data16: Meta Pixel events — when marketing consent is granted, the Meta Pixel fires a PageView event on every page load. This sends the page URL, referrer, and a browser identifier (_fbp cookie) to Meta's servers.
cookiePolicy.data17: Ad click identifiers — if you arrived from a Facebook/Instagram ad, the fbclid parameter from the URL is captured and stored in the _fbc cookie. This links your website visit to the specific ad you clicked.
cookiePolicy.data18: Cross-site identifiers — Meta may set third-party cookies (e.g., fr on facebook.com) that can link your visit to our site with your activity on Facebook, Instagram, and other sites that use Meta Pixel. This is how Meta builds advertising audiences.
cookiePolicy.data19: IP address (by Meta) — Meta receives your IP address as part of the Pixel request. Meta uses it for geo-targeting and fraud detection, and may hash and store it.
cookiePolicy.data20: Browser fingerprint signals (by Meta) — user agent string, Accept-Language header, screen resolution, and timezone are sent with the Pixel request. Meta may use these to improve ad targeting accuracy.
cookiePolicy.dataNote: What we do NOT collect: We do not collect your name, email address, phone number, physical address, or any other directly identifiable personal information through cookies or tracking technologies. We do not use tracking technologies on any forms. We do not engage in browser fingerprinting on our own. We do not sell your data to anyone. The randomly generated identifiers in cookies are not linked to your real identity in our systems.
cookiePolicy.loadingText1: Before you make a consent choice, only strictly necessary components are loaded (site functionality and the consent management script).
cookiePolicy.loadingText2: Analytics and marketing technologies are not activated until you explicitly grant consent. No tracking scripts run, no third-party cookies are set, and no data is sent to analytics or advertising providers before your choice.
cookiePolicy.loadingText3: After you make your choice, the consent state is saved to localStorage and applied to all tags via Google Consent Mode v2. Here is exactly what happens for each consent scenario:
cookiePolicy.loadingScenario1: You reject all (or close the banner): Only the GTM container script runs. Google Consent Mode signals are set to 'denied'. GA4 may send cookieless pings (without identifiers) for basic measurement, but no cookies are set and no personal data is collected. Clarity and Meta Pixel are not loaded at all.
cookiePolicy.loadingScenario2: You accept analytics only: GA4 starts collecting data and sets _ga, _ga_*, _gid cookies. Microsoft Clarity loads and begins session recording with _clck and _clsk cookies. Meta Pixel is not loaded. No marketing data is sent.
cookiePolicy.loadingScenario3: You accept marketing only: Meta Pixel loads and sends PageView events, setting _fbp cookie. GA4 and Clarity remain in consent-denied mode (no analytics cookies set).
cookiePolicy.loadingScenario4: You accept all: All technologies load — GA4 with full analytics, Clarity with session recordings, and Meta Pixel with conversion tracking. All corresponding cookies are set.
cookiePolicy.manageText1: You can change your cookie and technology preferences at any time by clicking Cookie Settings in the site footer. This reopens the settings panel where you can enable, disable, or fully withdraw consent for analytics and marketing technologies.
cookiePolicy.manageText2: You can withdraw your consent just as easily as you gave it. When you withdraw consent, analytics and marketing cookies are deleted immediately and the corresponding scripts stop running.
cookiePolicy.manageText3: Your choice is respected across the entire website for the duration of the consent storage period.
cookiePolicy.browserText: You can also manage cookies through your browser settings. Most browsers let you block or delete cookies. Check your browser's help documentation for instructions. Note that blocking all cookies may affect site functionality.
cookiePolicy.retentionH2: How Long Your Consent Choice Is Stored
cookiePolicy.retentionText1: Your consent state is stored in your browser's localStorage under the key penger_consent. It includes your choices for each category (necessary, analytics, marketing), a timestamp, and the current policy version.
cookiePolicy.retentionText2: The consent state is valid for 12 months from the date of your last choice. After this period, the consent banner will reappear so you can review and update your preferences.
cookiePolicy.retentionText3: If the policy version changes (for example, when we add or remove services), the consent banner will be shown again regardless of the expiration period, so you can make an informed choice based on the updated policy.
cookiePolicy.thirdPartyText: When you consent to analytics or marketing technologies, data is processed by the following third-party providers. Each provider processes data under their own privacy policy. We do not sell or share your data with any other parties beyond those listed below.
cookiePolicy.thirdParty1: Google LLC (Google Tag Manager, Google Analytics 4) — headquartered in Mountain View, CA, USA. Processes analytics data on Google Cloud infrastructure. Data may be stored in the US and other countries where Google operates data centers. Google acts as a data processor on our behalf for GA4 data. — Privacy Policy | GA4 Data Practices
cookiePolicy.thirdParty2: Microsoft Corporation (Microsoft Clarity) — headquartered in Redmond, WA, USA. Session recording and heatmap data is processed on Microsoft Azure infrastructure. Microsoft may process data in the US, Europe, and other regions. — Privacy Statement | Clarity Cookie Consent
cookiePolicy.thirdParty3: Meta Platforms, Inc. (Meta Pixel) — headquartered in Menlo Park, CA, USA. Advertising and conversion data is sent to Meta's servers. Meta may use this data to improve its advertising products, including showing you targeted ads on Facebook and Instagram. Meta acts as an independent data controller for some of this data, meaning Meta determines its own purposes for processing. — Privacy Policy
cookiePolicy.thirdPartyNote: Important: Once data is sent to a third-party provider, it is subject to that provider's privacy policy and data handling practices, which are outside our direct control. We encourage you to read their policies. We have selected these providers carefully and configured them to minimize data collection where possible.
cookiePolicy.transferText1: Our website is hosted and operated from Europe. However, when you consent to analytics or marketing technologies, your data may be transferred to and processed in the United States and other countries where our third-party providers operate data centers.
cookiePolicy.transferText2: Specifically: Google (GA4, GTM) and Meta (Pixel) are US-based companies. Data sent to these services may cross international borders. These providers rely on the EU-US Data Privacy Framework, Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs), and other legal mechanisms for lawful data transfers.
cookiePolicy.transferText3: Microsoft (Clarity) processes data on Azure infrastructure which has data centers worldwide, including in the EU. Microsoft relies on SCCs and the EU-US Data Privacy Framework for transfers outside the EEA.
cookiePolicy.transferText4: By granting consent to analytics or marketing technologies, you acknowledge that your data may be transferred to and processed in countries outside your country of residence, including the United States, where data protection laws may differ from those in your jurisdiction.
cookiePolicy.rightsText: Depending on your location, you may have the following rights regarding data collected through cookies and similar technologies:
cookiePolicy.rights1: Right to withdraw consent — you can withdraw your consent at any time by clicking "Cookie Settings" in the footer. This is as easy as giving consent. When withdrawn, tracking stops immediately and cookies are deleted.
cookiePolicy.rights2: Right to information — you have the right to know what data is collected about you. This policy provides that information in full detail.
cookiePolicy.rights3: Right to deletion — you can delete all cookies at any time through your browser settings or by withdrawing consent (which automatically deletes analytics and marketing cookies).
cookiePolicy.rights4: Right to object — by not granting consent, you effectively exercise your right to object to analytics and marketing data processing. No data is collected without your explicit consent.
cookiePolicy.rightsNote: To exercise any of these rights or for any questions, contact us at contact@mypenger.com.
cookiePolicy.updatesText1: We may update this policy when we add or remove services, change data processing practices, or when regulations change.
cookiePolicy.updatesText2: Any updates will be posted on this page with a new policy version number and last updated date.
cookiePolicy.updatesText3: When the policy version changes, the consent banner will reappear so you can review the changes and update your preferences.
cookiePolicy.contactText: If you have questions about our use of cookies and similar technologies, or about this policy, contact us:
